rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Use assign statements in implementation scope only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Place AGREE contracts in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the context of the declarations.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the analyzed model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers to avoid resolution errors.
Implementation-level bindings in AGREE should be placed in component implementation scopes.
AGREE scalar type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid invalid or package-qualified type names.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the actual expression types (int, real, bool).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for expressions and `gua` for guarantees; avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
Constants and equations in AGREE must be declared with valid scalar types: int, real, or bool.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE are either AADL features or declared within the same annex block.
anti_patterns: Do not reference non-existent AADL subcomponents or ports in AGREE contracts without first validating their existence.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assign and eq there.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE unless they are explicitly defined in the model.
Do not declare AGREE helpers with invalid or mismatched types that cause resolution errors.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
